meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to track user engagement and ship your first production-ready app.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of feedback loops. By integrating lightweight analytics early, you transform 'guessing' into data-driven development. This checklist guides you through instrumenting your code using Antigravity and Claude Code, ensuring you see exactly how users interact with your features, fueling the motivation to ship.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: the initial spark of an idea, the feverish late-night coding sessions, and then, the inevitable silence. The codebase grows stale, the excitement wanes, and another project joins the graveyard of 'almost finished' apps. It isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of visibility.

When you build in the dark, you lose the signal that tells you your work matters. By treating analytics as a foundational architectural requirement rather than an afterthought, you create a tangible bridge between your code and real-world impact. Let’s turn your next project into a portfolio piece by building the telemetry that keeps you accountable and informed.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one user action that proves your app delivers value. This prevents feature creep and keeps development focused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements and suggest 3 high-signal user events that correlate with core value delivery. Propose a schema for these events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify key user engagement metrics based on project context'

steps.title: Instrument the Critical Path
steps.description: Inject tracking at the most vulnerable points of user friction, like sign-ups or primary feature triggers, to identify where users drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a wrapper function for event tracking that handles asynchronous dispatching without blocking the main UI thread.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/utils/analytics.ts --prompt 'Implement a non-blocking event tracker'

steps.title: Automate User Flow Visualization
steps.description: Use automated tools to map how users move through your app, ensuring your navigation logic matches user intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a visualization script that aggregates my event logs into a user funnel report showing conversion rates between steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a funnel visualization script using current event logs'

steps.title: Implement Error Tracking Alerts
steps.description: Unfinished projects often die because of silent bugs. Set up real-time alerts to catch failures before your users do.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an error boundary that captures stack traces and logs them to our analytics dashboard whenever a component crashes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx --prompt 'Add telemetry to error boundary'

steps.title: Establish the 'Ship' Feedback Loop
steps.description: Connect your dashboard to a daily summary notification. Seeing data move keeps momentum high and prevents abandonment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that summarizes daily active users and event counts, then formats them for a Slack or Discord webhook.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a daily analytics summary cron job'

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus exclusively on events that directly influence your North Star metric to avoid 'data paralysis'.
Use pseudonymous IDs for users to maintain privacy while still being able to track individual user journeys.
Set up your analytics dashboard on day one. Watching the first '1' appear on your chart is a massive psychological win.
Instrument your code for 'exit intent' to understand exactly why users leave your app before completing their task.
Treat your analytics schema like API documentation; keep it versioned so you don't break your data pipelines as your app evolves.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, share your dashboard link in our community Slack channel to get feedback on your event tracking schema from senior mentors.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy to stop the cycle of abandonment and finally ship your vision. Stop coding in the dark and start building with data-backed confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
